Name:
By All Means Necessary
(With KMD)
Notes:
(July 13, 1971) (Daniel Dumile is born)
(? ?, 1988) (KMD is formed as a graffiti crew)

Name: 🏆 By All Means Necessary [Album]
Notes: Original KMD debut album dating from around 1987, before the title was stolen by KRS-One for Boogie Down Productions. Was confirmed to be real by the actual Mr. Hood.

Name:
🏆The Therapist
(feat. Jade One/Rodan) (prod. DJ Subroc)
Notes: Throwaway dating back to 1987, making it one of the first songs MF DOOM recorded in his KMD days. Kickstarter crowdfund to release it was planned but taken down by DOOM's estate. Features Rodan when he went by the name "Jade One". Was attempted to be released by X-Ray before his untimely death. Rest in peace.

Name:
Mr. Hood
(as Zev Love X with KMD)
Notes:
(1988) (Zev Love X, DJ Subroc, & Jade 1 (Rodan) form KMD, with Jade 1 leaving soon after to focus on school, being replaced with Onyx the Birthstone Kid.)
(May 14, 1991) (KMD's debut album Mr. Hood releases commercially.)

Name: ⭐Scrambled Eggs
Notes: Late KMD era. from the Stretch & Bobbito show. Scrambled Eggs was originally made for Mr. Hood. They debated whether having it as a bonus track or as the last song on Mr. Hood.

Name:
BL_CK B_ST_RDS
(With KMD)
Notes:
(March ?, 1993) (Black Bastards starts production)
(April 23, 1993) (DJ Subroc dies while attempting to cross the 878 Nassau Expressway)
(May ?, 1993 - August ?, 1993) (Zev Love X would finish up the album)
(May ?, 1994) (Black Bastards was supposed to release but didn't cause of Elektra dropping them due to the cover art)
(May 15, 2000) (Black Bastards would officially release)

Name: BL_CK B_ST_RDS Promo Tape [Album]
Notes: The advance copy of the album. Made in 1994.

Name: 🥇??? [Beat Tape]
Notes: Snippets from a KMD beat tape produced by DJ Subroc, included Smokin’ That $hit and Scrambled Eggz, among other beats. At one point the idea was to mix and master and utilize friends of KMD (Kurious, Grimm, Cage, Beatnuts, Del, and a couple of others) to rhyme over these tracks. The only track that leaked on here is an early instrumental version of What A Nigga Know? Snippet was posted on a now private or deleted instagram account

Name:
Ace In The Hole [V1]
(Prod. Subroc)
Notes: Demo version of Ace In The Hole by 3rd Bass, which was just the instrumental's bass loop and drums. Confirmed by Pete Nice in an interview.

Name:
What A Nigga Know? [V1] (Instrumental)
(prod. DJ Subroc)
(What A Niggy Know?)
Notes: Appears on a KMD beat tape produced by DJ Subroc. Posted to a Bandcamp page under the name of Dead Roach. Was uploaded by someone named Kevin, who was looking to get it remastered by Bob Ludwig. Assumably more tracks would be posted according to the header text on the archived page.

Name: Mental Illness
Notes: (September ?, 1994) (Zev Love X would take a break from the hip hop scene until 1997, where he would then meet start to record Operation: DOOMSDAY in MF Grimm's basement.)

Name:
🏆Mental Illness [Album]
(A Crack in Time)
Notes: KMD LP that was planned and worked on a bit after Black Bastards was intended to be released, but ended up being scrapped ater Subroc was tragically killed by being hit by a car. Was said to not feature Onyx but instead be a solo DOOM album under Zev Love X with different personalities. Only track known to be from the project is Sorcerers.

Name:
⭐Hell From Day One [V1]
(Gas Drawls)
Notes: A 1994 demo of Gas Drawls that was done when he was still known as Zev Love X. Titled "Hell From Day One" according to Westerly Touring, who had worked with Doom take it with a grain of salt though :sob:

Name:
Earthquake - Checkmate 
(Feat. MF DOOM) 
(Prod. Stretch Armstrong)
Notes: A demo dated from 1994 from underground rapper Earthquake. Has production from Stretch Armstrong.

Name: Unknown [Earthquake Collaborations]
Notes: MF DOOM may have had sessions with Earthquake, judging by the amount of songs they made and him saying, "We tried to do for Quake what Search did to us".

Name: Operation: Doomsday
Notes:
(May-Sept 1994) (The cancellation of the release of his & KMD's record combined with an overall loss of passion for hip-hop as a whole causes Zev Love X to step away from the hip-hop scene.)
(October 19, 1997) (Having comeback from his multi-year hiatus, Daniel Dumile has created a new character, MF DOOM, & releases his debut album under the name.)

Name: ? feat. Kurious (5:15 Version)
Notes: Confirmed by a tweet by DJ Stretch Armstrong. Unknown if there's more to the song, file name is "MF Doom Shit-NORM 37-60.m4a". In the tweet linked is an image alongside the video snippet, showcasing more early/placeholder names for other tracks. More in the row below.

Name: (Various tracks)
Notes:
DJ Stretch Armstrong posted an image (along with a short video) showcasing earlier names for several tracks,
MF Doom Shit-NORM 37-60 = ? feat. Kurious (5:15 Version)
The Elite Scooby Doo Shit = Hey!
Greenbacks Green Stacks = Greenbacks
Feel The Magnetism = The Mic
Blow Up the Dome = Gas Drawls (Speculated)

Name: Back In The Days

Name: Benzoin Gum

(Feat. Megalon) 
(Rhymes Like Dimes)
Notes: Original version of Rhymes Like Dimes with an alternate DOOM take and a Megalon verse. Has a different, more laid back beat and has alternate lyrics. Cuts randomly, so we probably don't have the full version. Doesn't feature DJ Cucumber Slice.

Name: The Finest [V1]
Notes: dj_git_hyper hanging out with DOOM in Brooklyn, capturing this footage of him recording “The Finest” in Jeff Brown’s apartment. As he already knew Doom for his KMD legacy, Git Hyper started filming him while he was spitting on the instrumentals (he brought with him) while Jeff tried to adjust the knobs.

Name: 🏆??? [Album]
Notes: J Dilla and MF DOOM collab album recorded late 2005, and postponed in February of 2006 due to J dilla's untimely passing. To start off the album's production, Dilla gave DOOM two beat CDs, with beats from Donuts being included on it. Few tracks were recorded, however we know "Sniper Elite" was one of them. In an interview with Bonafide, DOOM described the album's creation process as him and Dilla having "Telepathy" in the studio. A few of the beats intended for the project have been released officially.

LIGHTWORKS [V1]
(Prod. J Dilla)

(Hot Tea, The World Is Yours)

🏆Retarded Fren [V1]
(MDVLNZ 23)
Notes: Retarded Fren demo that was recorded in the Madvilliany 2 sessions. Has an alternate line mentioning the late J Dilla, so was likely recorded after his passing in 2005. Was later scrapped when DOOM was deported to England, before being rerecorded and released for Keys To The Kuffs. This demo uses the Jaylib instrumental from from The Mission (Stringed Out Mix). See edits tab for recreation

Name: Savior Beans [V1]
Notes: Original version of Savior Beans without J.Rocc's sound effects along with being uncenscord, the original line goes "Pooted it, shitted it in my bathroom". (😭) Also has the full outro instead of being cut off randomly

Name: ⭐Savior Beans [V2]
Notes: Played by J.Rocc on BBC 1Xtra, also appeared the bootleg DOOM! mixtape. Censored version of it most likely made for radio.

Name:
⭐Travis 911
(MDVLNZ 27)
Notes: Travis 911 was confirmed by DOOM to have the phone call at the end. Recorded past Jul 16, 2009 due to it being encoded with iTunes 8.2.1. Assumed to be part of a gapless playlist/album due to it having iTubesPGAP tag. The Chiba Chiba sample was reused on 2 tracks by Madlib; Strong Arm Steady's Cheeba Cheeba and Oh No's Give N Take. Features a prominent sample from "Lady Turk" by Tritonus. Track 27 on MV2 based on the file name. Actual bitrate of the track is around 175 kbps or so

Name:
✨️Guilty Simpson - Flower Called Trendsetters [V1]
(Prod. Madlib, Sterolab)
(MDVLNZ 23, A Flower Called Nowhere, Sterolab Flip)
Notes: Original version of "Trendsetters" by Guilty Simpson that uses the MDVLNZ 23 beat before DOOM got on it. Was played by J. Rocc however the full version is yet to be found. Uses an alternate flip of the beat.
Link(s): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bagIImYd94g sorry pillowcase doesnt work for me rn - fawf

Name:
⭐🏆A Flower Called Nowhere [V2]*
(Prod. Madlib, Stereolab)
(MDVLNZ 23, A Flower Called Nowhere, Stereolab Flip, Flower Called Trendsetters)
Notes: Madvillainy 2 song sampling Stereolabs' "The Flower Called Nowhere". The instrumental has been showcase many times by Madlib, however the version with vocals is a grail. Was track twenty-three on a Madvillainy 2 tracklist. Also known as "Stereolab Flip" and "A Flower Called Nowhere" OG file is somewhere along the lines of "MDVLNZ 23", however the exact filename is unknown. Was made before 2015, as it was played at the Redbull Music Academy according to a 2015 interview with Stereolab singer Lætitia Sadier herself. Track 23 on MV2 based on it's file name.

Name:
⭐ Bottles Like Superstar
(feat. Mr. Fantastik)
(In The Club, Posted Up)
Notes: Was found on a now deleted Soundcloud page and reposted to Youtube. Has an open verse, signifying it's a rough demo. Uses the instrumental from Madlib's remix of Redman, Method Man, and Sheek Louch's 2 Tears In A Bucket called "Flyby" from Madlib Remixes Part 2. Samples "Angel Eyes" by Bar Kays. Referred to by fans as "Posted Up" or "In The Club", however it's official name is unknown. The file sounds decent enough, however the actual bitrate of the music is only 96 kbps

It was described as amazing, however it was never recorded on any camera or phone. Despite this, the files still might be had. this was also the same interview where she had confirmed she was on madvillainy 2 BUT I FUCKED UP!!!

Name: ✨ MF DOOM & Trunks - Unicron [Album]
Notes:
Unicron is the only collaboration between American rappers MF DOOM and Trunks. The EP is produced by MF DOOM and features Trunks' rhyming. The album came into fruition after Trunks was featured in the song "Lockjaw" off of DOOM's album Take Me to Your Leader, under the alias King Geedorah. "Lockjaw" also appears in Unicron as track 3.

Unicron was never commercially released until 2022, instead it was made available as a bootleg on many websites. In 2022 De Rap Winkel released the EP as several numbered limited editions on vinyl.

Era: To make your own tracker, click File, Make Copy and save it to your drive. Then you can just edit in the proper information. Read the information below to avoid errors
Notes: Notable Fan Projects: gather community opinions, learn how to use mobile sheets (:sob:)
Leak Date: Communicate other various notes with Editors here

Era: To add a new album/era, right click "A" and click conditional formatting . Edit the eras or add new ones. You also have to select A2-A(End of Tracker), right click and select Data Validation and add the era name to the list. Otherwise you will get a red triangle error in the corner. You cannot include cell A1 in the Data Validation, also don't include cells below the tracker. Including A1 and cells below the tracker for conditional formatting is fine.
Leak Date: all imgur links replaced with the exception of 1 file that didnt back up for some reason

Era: Ordering Scheme (please make amendments as you see fit): First is all mixtapes/collections, then all leaks put into alphabetical order. Then put all leaks that feature Kanye after that, in alphabetical order of the artist's names. If there are multiple versions of a song, put the earliest versions of the song first, if that's unknown order them by release/leak date.
No fan remakes, no unofficial mixtapes, nothing that's commercially available, always upload to a site that can be streamed off of if Dbree goes down.
Make sure you use alternating colors and check often that they are not broken (Format, Alternating Colors, choose white and the grey that has one grey in between it and white)
No released remixes, no matter how obscure, should be on this page. Those should be in Misc / Released Tracker.
All music video editions, live freestyles, and released remixes go in Misc / Released Tracker.

New eras are only allowed to be made if it follows at least 3 of the 4 criteria: (1) has a some sort of song list associated with it, (2) has 20 or more unreleased tracks, (3) has a timeframe of over a month, (4) has an exact start and end date. Collab/group album with 5 or more unreleased tracks do not have to follow the criteria.
Whenever you add something new, or we get a new snippet or leak of something, be sure to have it bordered in yellow for a week.
If it isn't a new confirmed leak or snippet, always ask other editors before adding an entry.
You can change these rules however you like to fit your tracker.
